title: Evas structural composition
wikitext:
[]: Eva's structural composition             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Sat Aug 12, 2006 1:23 am We all know that an Eva unit is a biomachanical creature. You add armour to it, and it goes out and fight. Just like a legionnaire. I wonder, where and how do you find the flesh to make those Eva Unit? And what is the chemical composition of Eva's body? Her blood? Those purple fluid. And what is her armour made of that make it able to withstand a direct hit from a positron rifle?

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Sat Aug 12, 2006 4:14 am I'm not really sure if any of those things have canonical answers, but I'm guessing that armour is some heavy duty stuff bolstered by the presence of the AT Field. I was always to believe the flesh on an Eva wasn't applied, per say, since the Evas are basically grown, right? Grown and then modified when fully grown. I guess to get the Eva to do what they wanted it to, some pretty horrible stuff went down in preparation. In the graveyard/NERV flashback scenes, they don't seem especially humanoid in appearance, with long spines and the legs. Never quite understood how that works. Anyone got an answer? A link to a huge forum post debating/explaining this would be sublime.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Aug 12, 2006 11:11 am As far as I could tell, the Evas in the graveyard were severely deformed. That's probably why they were scrapped; Units 00 and 01 must have been the first ones to actually be usable. But then one must ask why all the dead pre-Evas were earing helmets like Unit 00's.

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Sat Aug 12, 2006 2:49 pm BobBQ wrote: But then one must ask why all the dead pre-Evas were wearing helmets like Unit 00's.

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Sat Aug 12, 2006 4:35 pm Well, the first component is the Eva's actual "natural" body, which is grown/cloned using genetic material from Adam (with slight modifications that vary from Eva to Eva). Basically just a giant human body, bones, organs, muscles, skin and all. You can see Eva-01 in this state while she's still atached to Lilith (she was born/grown from Lilith somehow, the details on how the others were grown are left unknown). After this, most of the spinal column is removed, and replaced with a prosthetic spinal column designed to house the Entry Plug and all that nasty stuff. Once that's done, you can have your Contact Experiment, and give your Eva a soul to make her functional. Then there's the matter of the armor, restraints and such. If the scene where Eva-01 pops off her breastplate in Episode 19 is any indication, it looks like they might actually have frames, attachment points for armor and such actually bolted or fused to their skin or skeleton. Ouch. The blood is just blood, naturally produced by the Eva's body as far as I can tell. Eva-02's the only one who has the purpleish blood, and from an artistic standpoint that's so that it can stand out against her red armor. From an in-show standpoint, I guess some genetic property of her blood was tweaked that ended up making it that color.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Aug 12, 2006 4:58 pm felineki wrote: I guess some genetic property of her blood was tweaked that ended up making it that color.

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Sat Aug 12, 2006 5:53 pm felineki wrote: Well, the first component is the Eva's actual "natural" body, which is grown/cloned using genetic material from Adam (with slight modifications that vary from Eva to Eva). Basically just a giant human body, bones, organs, muscles, skin and all. You can see Eva-01 in this state while she's still atached to Lilith (she was born/grown from Lilith somehow, the details on how the others were grown are left unknown). After this, most of the spinal column is removed, and replaced with a prosthetic spinal column designed to house the Entry Plug and all that nasty stuff. Once that's done, you can have your Contact Experiment, and give your Eva a soul to make her functional. Then there's the matter of the armor, restraints and such. If the scene where Eva-01 pops off her breastplate in Episode 19 is any indication, it looks like they might actually have frames, attachment points for armor and such actually bolted or fused to their skin or skeleton. Ouch.

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Sat Aug 12, 2006 6:04 pm the3rdchildren wrote: That all works for me, grotesque as it sounds.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 13, 2006 2:49 am felineki wrote: You can see Eva-01 in this state while she's still atached to Lilith (she was born/grown from Lilith somehow,

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Sun Aug 13, 2006 9:43 am Well if the body parts of the Eva can grow by itself, why do you need electricity to activate it?

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Sun Aug 13, 2006 2:10 pm Reichu wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by Ripple_in_Eternity » Wed Aug 16, 2006 4:00 pm tinmeigut wrote: Well if the body parts of the Eva can grow by itself, why do you need electricity to activate it?

Article: Evas structural composition
4:35 pm Well, the first component is the Eva's actual "natural" body, which is grown/cloned using genetic material from Adam (with slight modifications that vary from Eva to Eva). Basically just a giant human body, bones, organs, muscles, skin and all. You can see Eva-01 in this state while she's still atached to Lilith (she was born/grown from Lilith somehow, the details on how the others were grown are left unknown). After this, most of the spinal column is removed, and replaced with a prosthetic spinal column designed to house the Entry Plug and all that nasty stuff. Once that's done, you can have your Contact Experiment, and give your Eva a soul to make her functional. Then there's the matter of the armor, restraints and such. If the scene where Eva-01 pops off her breastplate in Episode 19 is any indication, it looks like they might actually have frames, attachment points for armor and such actually bolted or fused to their skin or skeleton. Ouch. The blood is just blood, naturally produced by the Eva's body as far as I can tell. Eva-02's the only one who has the purpleish blood, and from an artistic standpoint that's so that it can stand out against her red armor. From an in-show standpoint, I guess some genetic property of her blood was tweaked that ended up making it that color.
